raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that counts lines with alphanumeric symbols in all .php files under /testbed. Use grep -E -c? We need total number across all files. Use grep -E -o? Actually count lines that contain at least one alphanumeric? Use grep -E -c '[[:alnum:]]' per file and sum. Use grep -E -c '[[:alnum:]]' *.php? But recursive. Use grep -R -E -c? But -c per file. We can use grep -R -E '[[:alnum:]]' /testbed/*.php? But need count lines. Use grep -R -E '[[:alnum:]]' /testbed/*.php | wc -l. That counts matching lines across all files. So command: grep -R -E '[[:alnum:]]' /testbed/*.php | wc -l. But need to include subfolders: use find. grep -R works. But pattern may match binary? Use grep -R -E '[[:alnum:]]' /testbed/*.php? Actually glob won't recurse. Use grep -R -E '[[:alnum:]]' /testbed/*.php? Better: grep -R -E '[[:alnum:]]' /testbed/*.php? Might not find subfolders. Use grep -R -E '[[:alnum:]]' /testbed/*.php? Actually -R will search recursively but pattern only .php? Use grep -R -E '[[:alnum:]]' --include='*.php' /testbed | wc -l. That's good.

task.query: Counts total number of only lines with alphanumeric symbols in all *.php files in the /testbed folder and subfolders.
task.gold: find /testbed -name '*.php' | xargs cat | awk '/[a-zA-Z0-9]/ {i++} END{print i}'

record.query: Counts total number of only lines with alphanumeric symbols in all *.php files in the /testbed folder and subfolders.
record.gold: find /testbed -name '*.php' | xargs cat | awk '/[a-zA-Z0-9]/ {i++} END{print i}'

reset.observation: Counts total number of only lines with alphanumeric symbols in all *.php files in the /testbed folder and subfolders.
